cases.name: Anchored EVM Merkle release settles against the official escrow lane
cases.notes: The runtime devnet test creates escrow, publishes the checkpoint root, verifies inclusion, and releases funds through the Merkle-proof path.

cases.name: Expired escrows refund deterministically and project timeout state back into ARC truth
cases.notes: Timeout recovery remains explicit and fail closed rather than leaving funds locked without ARC-side lifecycle projection.

cases.name: Dual-signature settlement remains operator-bound and statically verifiable
cases.notes: The runtime derives the contract digest, signs with the registered settlement key, and validates the release path before submission.

cases.name: Bond lock, release, impairment, expiry, and observation remain explicit
cases.notes: The runtime exposes bounded bond transaction builders plus lifecycle observation instead of hiding reserve posture in off-chain bookkeeping.

cases.name: Solana-native Ed25519 settlement preparation preserves commitment parity with the EVM lane
cases.notes: The Solana lane verifies ARC receipt signatures and compares settlement commitments instead of claiming unaudited cross-chain equivalence.

cases.name: Confirmation, dispute-window, and reorg recovery remain visible and bounded
cases.notes: The observer emits explicit finality state plus recovery actions for wait, retry, refund, or manual review.

cases.name: Merkle and Solana settlement lanes require durable receipts plus kernel-signed checkpoints
cases.notes: The direct runtime rejects disabled checkpoint statements, mismatched signer assumptions, and non-durable evidence inputs instead of attempting release from incomplete receipt truth.

cases.name: Generated E2E qualification proves FX-backed dual-sign settlement plus recovery posture for refund, reorg, impair, and expiry
cases.notes: The dedicated `./scripts/qualify-web3-e2e.sh` lane writes `target/web3-e2e-qualification/partner-qualification.json` plus per-scenario reports so hosted reviewers can inspect one bounded settlement proof package instead of inferring it from separate devnet tests.
